title: Climate Change and Extreme Weather
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The (0) frequency of extreme weather events has risen sharply over recent decades. Scientists attribute this largely to human-caused (17) environmental changes, particularly the release of greenhouse gases. Rising temperatures are making storms and floods increasingly (18) destructive.||Unless governments take action to reduce carbon emissions quickly, the consequences could be (19) catastrophic. Communities in (20) vulnerable regions will need (21) considerable support to adapt. It is also important to raise (22) awareness among the public so that people understand the (23) urgency of addressing this global challenge before it is too late. Individual (24) responsibility must also play a part in reducing emissions.

example.text: The (0) FREQUENCY of extreme weather events has risen sharply over recent decades. (FREQUENT)

questions.explanation: The adjective 'environmental' is formed from 'environment' by adding the suffix '-al'.
The adjective 'destructive' comes from the root 'destruct' with the suffix '-ive'.
The adjective 'catastrophic' is formed from 'catastrophe' by replacing '-e' with '-ic'.
The adjective 'vulnerable' describes being exposed to harm; formed with the suffix '-able'.
The adjective 'considerable' is formed from 'consider' by adding the suffix '-able'.
The noun 'awareness' is formed from 'aware' by adding the suffix '-ness'.
The noun 'urgency' is formed from 'urgent' by replacing '-ent' with '-ency'.
The noun 'responsibility' is formed from 'responsible' by changing '-ble' to '-bility'.
